title: Neumann-Lara / Škrekovski Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: If $G$ is a planar graph, then $\vec{\chi}(G) \leq 2$.
context_text: In analogy to the Four-Colour-Theorem, this conjecture was proposed by Neumann-Lara [NL85] and independently by Škrekovski [BFJ+04]. The paper's main result (Theorem 2) shows this conjecture is equivalent to the statement that all oriented $K_5$-minor-free graphs are 2-colourable.
paper_contribution: Shows that Neumann-Lara's Conjecture is equivalent to the more general statement that every $K_5$-minor-free graph $G$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(G) \leq 2$ and moreover any orientation of $G$ admits an acyclic 2-colouring without monochromatic triangles.
paper_title: A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2

title: Question 1
statement_text: What is the largest minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}_2$ of undirected graphs with dichromatic number at most 2?
context_text: The authors note that while $\vec{\chi}(K_6) = 2$ and $\vec{\chi}(K_7) = 3$, so $\mathcal{G}_2$ is a subclass of the $K_7$-minor-free graphs, it seems to be much smaller: there exist $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs with dichromatic number greater than 2.
paper_title: A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2
